mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpaces demand during supply-constrained periods, easing inflationary pressures and allowing equity markets to rebound, generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive SPX returns during high-inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL > 300), with mean forward return < 0.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive policy rates and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary risk premium, forcing investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations when both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and UNRATE > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and cash flows, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 300 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 300.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside stable or declining unemployment confirms a healthy labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending capacity and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.
